responses.1: We encrypt databases using industry standard methods. Some legacy systems use older encryption but we are in the process of upgrading.
responses.2: Our web applications support TLS but some internal APIs still use HTTP for performance reasons. We are planning to address this.
responses.3: We comply with applicable privacy laws. We have a privacy policy on our website and train staff annually on data handling.
responses.4: Data is retained as required by law. We delete data when no longer needed. Specific timelines depend on the data type.
responses.5: We aim to restore services as quickly as possible following an outage. We do not have formally defined RTO/RPO commitments at this time.
responses.6: We test our business continuity plan periodically. Our last test was approximately 18 months ago.
responses.7: We back up data nightly. Backups are stored on-site and also on cloud storage. We have not tested restoration recently.
responses.8: We use some third-party services. We have standard contracts in place with most of our suppliers.
responses.9: We review our key suppliers annually through a standard procurement process. Security is one of the factors reviewed.
responses.10: We do not currently hold ISO 27001 or SOC 2 certification. We follow industry best practices and conduct internal security reviews.
responses.11: We maintain compliance with relevant regulations in our primary jurisdiction. Our legal team monitors regulatory developments.
responses.12: We will notify clients of security incidents as required by applicable law and our contractual obligations. Timelines depend on the nature of the incident.
responses.13: We have an incident response process. Our IT team handles security incidents. We have procedures documented internally.
responses.14: We have experienced some minor security events over the past 2 years which were handled internally. We do not believe these rose to the level of material incidents requiring customer notification.
